title: alyssum purpureum
intro: Alyssum purpureum, also known as purple alyssum or madwort, is a small, annual or biennial flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Alyssum purpureum include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture, infusion, or topical cream, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
